name: SouthTrust Corporation
description: SouthTrust Corporation was a prominent American banking company headquartered in Birmingham, Alabama. Established in 1887, it grew to become a significant super-regional bank with hundreds of offices across several states. The corporation provided a wide range of financial services, including commercial and retail banking, trust services, and investment management. In 2004, SouthTrust Corporation entered into a merger agreement with Wachovia Corporation, which was subsequently acquired by Wells Fargo. As a result of these corporate consolidations, SouthTrust ceased to exist as an independent entity, and its operations were fully integrated into the larger banking organizations.
